posts.title: server building
posts.text: configurations system installing service running/been ran on it configurations# processor i3-7100T35w Since servr have to run 7*24, it should be power-saving. Otherwise it would be a large eletricity bill to pay. motherboard 昂达 B365SD3-ITX This is really not the best motherboard to use, DDR3 runs at a lower speed and when compared to DDR4 it only have slight price adventage. But I have two 4g DDR3 RAM, so I choice this to suit with 7 gen cpu and DDR3 at the same time. RAM DDR3 4g * **2 镁光 power supply 300w case 银欣ITX cooling system IDCOOLING IS-40X 4 heatpipePHANTEKS MG SKIRON 12cm hard drive 2.5mm 2T Seagate SMR (quit using as soon as possible, when I can afford two PMR to build a raid array) Toshiba 4T * 2 5400rpm (raid 1) ssd 杂牌64G SATA（when SMART report it was danger to continue using, I change it into the Lexar I’m using now） Lexar nm100 SATA 128G system installing# Ubuntu 20.04LTS (place in the living room used both as a media player for my parents and a 24*7 was) OMV (when I found my parents seldom use it, I change into OMV for more convenient raid experience) FreeNAS (freeBSD need VM to run docker, when you are using a i3 processor it really drives people crazy) OMV (yes, I’m back) TrueNAS scale (useable ZFS+ Debian + docker, this is the dream NAS OS) service running/been ran on it# Nextcloud (appreciate by my mum so she don’t need to use WeChat or flash drive to keep her documents synchronised) Jellyfin (use aria2 to download movie and Jellyfin to manage them is a good choice) No-ip (DDNS + Dynamic external IP is a solution to access it when you are at school, though expose you NAS can be danger for you data, so make sure you know what you are doing) PhotoPrime (google open-source gallery, use tenser flow to classify photos, after using iCloud this had been aban) Aria2 pro (best aria2 docker ever found, move when complete, auto update tracker-list and auto delete temp file when complete)
